_note: Chapter 7 — Gentry taps the aleph through the Count. The gray house. The cost.

c7_bench.name: The Count's Corner
c7_bench.desc: A week of vigil has made the corner a ward: stretcher, drip, Cherry's shoebox telemetry pulsing amber. The Count's eyes move under their lids like weather on another planet. The gray slab is warm to the touch. You've stopped touching it.

c7_ground.name: Factory — Ground Floor
c7_ground.desc: Torchlight and machine skeletons. The Judge stands half-rebuilt where you left it, one arm raised like a question you haven't answered. Overhead, Gentry's loft burns its lonely light.

c7_generator.name: Generator Shed
c7_generator.desc: The Factory's heart: a scavenged fusion cell and a prayer of cabling. The aleph tap draws more every night. When the needles dip, somewhere inside a universe, it is dreaming harder.

c7_catwalk.name: High Catwalk
c7_catwalk.desc: Wind through broken lights, the Solitude spread black below. On the interstate, kilometers out: headlights in convoy formation, holding steady, patient. Coming here. Nobody drives to Dog Solitude by accident.

c7_loft.name: Gentry's Loft — the Rig
c7_loft.desc: Every screen Gentry owns is turned to the tap like flowers to a black sun. He's strung a second trode set — yours. 'You hold the line,' he says, eyes enormous. 'I read the Shape. And Slick — whatever speaks in there, we do NOT insult it.'
